# services.chrony

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.chrony`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.chrony.autotrimThreshold` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | Maximum estimated error threshold for the `rtcautotrim` command. When reached, the RTC will be trimmed. Only used when [](#opt-services.chrony.enableRTCTrimming) is enabled. |
| `services.chrony.directory` | `string` | Directory where chrony state is stored. |
| `services.chrony.dispatcherScript` | `boolean` | Whether to install the chrony NetworkManager dispatcher script to handle connectivity changes. |
| `services.chrony.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to synchronise your machine's time using chrony. Make sure you disable NTP if you enable this service. |
| `services.chrony.enableMemoryLocking` | `boolean` | Whether to add the `-m` flag to lock memory. |
| `services.chrony.enableNTS` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Network Time Security authentication. Make sure it is supported by your selected NTP server(s). |
| `services.chrony.enableRTCTrimming` | `boolean` | Enable tracking of the RTC offset to the system clock and automatic trimming. See also [](#opt-services.chrony.autotrimThreshold) ::: {.note} This is not compatible with the `rtcsync` directive, which naively syncs the RTC time every 11 minutes. Tracking the RTC drift will allow more precise timekeeping, especially on intermittently running devices, where the RTC is very relevant. ::: |
| `services.chrony.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration directives that should be added to {file}`chrony.conf` |
| `services.chrony.extraFlags` | `list of string` | Extra flags passed to the chronyd command. |
| `services.chrony.initstepslew.enabled` | `boolean` | DEPRECATED. Consider using `services.chrony.makestep` instead. Allow chronyd to make a rapid measurement of the system clock error at boot time, and to correct the system clock by stepping before normal operation begins. |
| `services.chrony.initstepslew.threshold` | `floating point number or signed integer` | The threshold of system clock error (in seconds) above which the clock will be stepped. If the correction required is less than the threshold, a slew is used instead. |
| `services.chrony.makestep.enable` | `boolean` | Allow chronyd to step the system clock if the error is larger than the specified threshold. |
| `services.chrony.makestep.limit` | `positive integer, meaning >0` | The maximum number of times the system clock will be stepped. |
| `services.chrony.makestep.threshold` | `floating point number or signed integer` | The threshold of system clock error (in seconds) above which the clock will be stepped. If the correction required is less than the threshold, a slew is used instead. |
| `services.chrony.package` | `package` | The chrony package to use. |
| `services.chrony.serverOption` | `one of "iburst", "offline"` | Set option for server directives. Use "iburst" to rapidly poll on startup. Recommended if your machine is consistently online. Use "offline" to prevent polling on startup. Recommended if your machine boots offline or is otherwise frequently offline. |
| `services.chrony.servers` | `list of string` | The set of NTP servers from which to synchronise. |
